name: Chocolate Gingerbread with Bittersweet Glaze
recipeInstructions:
1. In a double boiler, melt chocolate, molasses, oil, and butter over low heat until smooth. Set aside.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
3. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, brown sugar, baking soda, baking powder, spices, salt, and white pepper.
4. In a small bowl, beat eggs with a whisk until foamy. Add buttermilk and vanilla.
5. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and pour in the egg-buttermilk mixture. Stir to combine.
6. Gradually add chocolate mixture to the batter, stirring until smooth.
7. Pour batter into a greased 9-inch springform pan.
8. Bake for 35-40 minutes in the preheated oven until the top springs back when touched and a cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Set the cake on a rack and remove the springform sides.
10. Prepare bittersweet glaze by melting butter, chocolate, and corn syrup over low heat in a double boiler until smooth.
11. Pour the glaze over the warm cake, allowing it to drip down the sides. You can also spread the glaze with a knife if desired.
12. Cool the cake on a rack until ready to serve.
Bittersweet Glaze:
6 tablespoons unsalted butter
4 1/2 ounces bittersweet or semisweet chocolate
1 tablespoon light corn syrup
Combine butter, chocolate, and corn syrup in a double boiler over low heat and stir until smooth.
